description_long: This object is a stylized daisy-like flower with a prominent, rounded yellow center and numerous elongated white petals radiating outward in a single layer. The petals are slightly curved and overlap at their bases, creating a dense, symmetrical whorl around the central disk. The yellow center is smooth and dome-shaped, contrasting with the flat, matte texture of the petals. There are no visible leaves or stem attached to the flower, giving it a floating appearance. The petals display subtle variations in shading, with some grayish areas suggesting shadow or stylization. The overall form is flat and circular, resembling a classic daisy blossom often found in urban parks or decorative landscaping.
description: A flat, daisy-like flower with a yellow dome center and many white, slightly overlapping petals arranged in a circular whorl. The flower lacks a visible stem or leaves and has a stylized, symmetrical appearance.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a low angle, highlighting the yellow center and the layered, outward-radiating white petals.
description_view_1: This view shows the flower from a slightly different angle, emphasizing the smooth dome of the yellow center and the even spread of the petals.
description_view_2: The flower appears flatter in this view, with the petals forming a near-perfect circle around the yellow center.
description_view_3: From this angle, the symmetry of the petal arrangement is most apparent, and the subtle shading on the petals is visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, forming a single, symmetrical layer around the central disk. The bases of the petals overlap slightly, creating a dense and uniform circular whorl typical of many daisy species.
